solution: 
```cpp
class LUPrefix {
private:
    vector<int> uploaded;
    int longestPrefix;
public:
    LUPrefix(int n) : uploaded(n + 1, 0), longestPrefix(0) {
        // Initialize uploaded array with 0s (not uploaded)
    }
    
    void upload(int video) {
        uploaded[video] = 1;
        // If the uploaded video is the next in the prefix, update the longest prefix
        if (video == longestPrefix + 1) {
            // Find the new longest prefix by checking consecutive uploaded videos
            int newLongest = longestPrefix;
            while (newLongest + 1 < uploaded.size() && uploaded[newLongest + 1] == 1) {
                newLongest++;
            }
            longestPrefix = newLongest;
        }
    }
    
    int longest() {
        return longestPrefix;
    }
};

/**
 * Your LUPrefix object will be instantiated and called as such:
 * LUPrefix* obj = new LUPrefix(n);
 * obj->upload(video);
 * int param_2 = obj->longest();
 */
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1) for upload and longest in average cases, O(n) in worst-case scenarios when the prefix is extended
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses an array to track uploaded videos and maintains the longest uploaded prefix. When a video is uploaded, it checks if it extends the current prefix and updates accordingly. This approach is efficient for most operations, with minimal overhead for tracking the prefix. The worst-case time complexity for upload is O(n) when the prefix is extended to the end, but this is rare in practice.
